title: Cycle affinity and winding localize eigenvalues of Markov generators
authors: Artemy Kolchinsky, Naruo Ohga, Sosuke Ito
abstract: The complex eigenvalues of Markov generators govern oscillatory properties of relaxation, autocorrelation, and linear response. Here we show that these eigenvalues are localized by nonequilibrium cycles of the generator, thus revealing a fundamental tradeoff between thermodynamic driving, oscillation, and decay of eigenmodes. Specifically, we prove that each complex eigenvalue is confined to a region determined by the cycle affinity and the eigenvector ``winding number'' of some nonequilibrium cycle. In unicyclic systems, we also demonstrate that the winding number coincides with the ordered eigenvalue index, yielding new thermodynamic bounds on the slowest and fastest relaxation modes. In multicyclic systems, our approach unifies and extends several previous inequalities and proves the Uhl--Seifert ellipse conjecture.
summary: This work establishes fundamental mathematical bounds on the complex eigenvalues of Markov generators by linking them to the system's non-equilibrium cycles. It proves that the oscillatory behavior of the system is constrained by a tradeoff between thermodynamic driving forces (cycle affinity) and the geometric winding number of the eigenvectors. This provides powerful, generalizable tools for analyzing relaxation dynamics in complex stochastic processes.
main_problem: To understand how complex eigenvalues of Markov generators, which govern oscillatory properties of relaxation and autocorrelation, are localized by non-equilibrium cycles.
main_result: The paper proves that complex eigenvalues are confined to regions determined by the cycle affinity and the eigenvector's winding number, establishing new thermodynamic bounds on relaxation modes.
method: The analysis uses spectral theory applied to continuous-time Markov chains, relating eigenvalue properties to cycle affinities and winding numbers via geometric and convex hull constructions.
model_or_system: The system is modeled by continuous-time Markov chains whose dynamics are governed by a rate matrix (generator) $R$. The analysis focuses on irreducible, unicyclic, and multicyclic generators.
key_observables: Complex eigenvalues ($\lambda$), Cycle Affinity ($F$), Winding Number ($\omega(u)$), and the relaxation/oscillation rates ($	ext{Re } \lambda, |	ext{Im } \lambda|$).
important_parameters: Cycle affinity ($F$), system size ($n$), and the parameter $	au \in [0, 1]$ used to interpolate between different decay regimes.
main_assumptions: The main theorems initially assume the generator is irreducible and unicyclic, though generalizations to multicyclic systems are presented.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the derived eigenvalue bounds, comparing the topology-dependent localization region (Theorem 4) against the simpler, topology-free region defined by the Farey sequence (Theorem 5).
paper_structure: The paper progresses from establishing bounds in unicyclic systems (Theorem 1 & 2), to generalizing these bounds to multicyclic systems (Theorem 3), and finally deriving two types of localization regions: one dependent on specific cycle topologies (Theorem 4) and one independent of topology (Theorem 5).
why_interesting: The connection between spectral properties (eigenvalues) and non-equilibrium thermodynamics (cycle affinity) provides powerful, quantitative bounds on relaxation dynamics, which is crucial for understanding open quantum systems and energy dissipation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Illustration of Theorem 1 for a unicyclic generator with maximum escape rate r and cycle affinity F ≥0. (a) Each eigen- value λ obeys the bounds (4) (solid brown) and (5) (dashed brown), which depend on the winding number w (equivalently, sorted eigen- value index). (b) Taking the union across all winding numbers w ∈{1, . . . , ⌈n/2⌉−1} gives a non-convex localization region for all eigenvalues (black outline). The green ellipse is the bound corresponding to the Uhl–Seifert conjecture [12]; our previous sector bound [22] is shown using magenta arrows.

FIG. 2. (a) Theorem 4 illustrated on a 6-state generator with 3 cycles: (nc, Fc) = (3, 4), (4, 4), (5, 16). Eigenvalues are localized to a topology- and affinity-dependent region (thick black), defined as a union of convex hulls (thin grey lines) induced by the 3 cycles (blue, yellow, and orange dots). We show four Topology-dependent bounds from Table I: τ = 1 (solid magenta), τ = 0 (dashed magenta), τ = −Re λ/2r (purple lines), and τ = 1/2 (Uhl-Seifert ellipse, green). (b) Theorem 5 specifies a topology-free localization region in terms of F∗:= maxc tanh(Fc/2nc) and size n = 6. It is the convex hull of points induced by Farey fractions (white dots).
